case_title: DARUKA WANIGATUNGA VS. THOMAS "TOBY" A. ROEBUCK ET AL

docket_entries.description: 170.6 PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE PENDING AS TO JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES (TRANSACTION ID # 75796051) FILED BY PLAINTIFF WANIGATUNGA, DARUKA

EX PARTE (1) APPLICATION FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER TO PREVENT SALE OF PLAINTIFF'S HOME AND (2) REQUEST FOR OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 75853648) FILED BY PLAINTIFF WANIGATUNGA, DARUKA
DECLARATION RE CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41 (TRANSACTION ID # 76074823) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROEBUCK, THOMAS "TOBY" A. AKA "TA ROEBUCK," THOMAS A. ROEBUCK 1992 RECOVABLE TRUST

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUCNTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS FOR FRAUD AND VIOLATION OF CIV. CODE SECTION 2924G(C)(2), AND THE APPLICABILITY OF CIV. CODE SECTION 2923.5 TO THIS DEED OF TRUST. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF, AS AN INDIVIDUAL, DOES NOT HAVE STANDING TO SUE. PLAINTIFFS ALSO FAILED TO ESTABLISH IRREPARABLE HARM. FINALLY, THE COURT NOTES PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A PROOF OF SERVICE OF THE MOVING PAPERS IN SUPPORT OF THE REQUEST FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, AS REQUIRED BY THE MARCH 14, 2025 ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: VICTOR M. HWANG ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: JEFFREY B. NEUSTADT'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (FOR DARUKA WANIGATUNGA)

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Michelle Tong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/MT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-04-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Continued To Oct-01-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUCNTION
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUCNTION is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show a likelihood of success on the merits for fraud and violation of Civ. Code Section 2924g(c)(2), and the applicability of Civ. Code Section 2923.5 to this deed of trust. The Court further notes that Plaintiff, as an individual, does not have standing to sue. Plaintiffs also failed to establish irreparable harm. Finally, the Court notes Plaintiff failed to file a proof of service of the moving papers in support of the request for a preliminary injunction and the summons and complaint, as required by the March 14, 2025 Order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Victor M. Hwang ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUCNTION
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Continued To May-27-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jeffrey Neustadt; 415-434-4440; Law Office of Jeffrey Neustadt; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Sarah Little; 510-763-1000; Kornfield, Nyberg, Bendes, Kuhner & Little, P.C.; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Counsel contests the date as he is not available. Both parties agree to another date. The Court rules as follows:

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUCNTION is CONTINUED TO May 27, 2025 at 9:30am on the courts own motion.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUCNTION
Off Calendar Apr-21-2025 Continued To May-01-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is CONTINUED to May 1, 2025 for Defendant to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the opposition no later than April 22, 2025 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The Court further notes that Plaintiff failed to file a proof of service by March 28, 2025 as ordered. Proof of service must be on file no later than April 22, 2025.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
